name: Easter Island (Rapa Nui)
summary: Easter Island, called Rapa Nui by the Polynesian people who live there, is one of the loneliest islands on Earth and famous for nearly 1,000 giant stone statues called moai.
funFacts: Easter Island sits in the Pacific Ocean about 3,500 kilometres from Chile, making it one of the most remote inhabited places in the whole world.
There are nearly 1,000 moai statues on the island, and the heaviest one ever raised onto a platform weighs about 86 tonnes, heavier than 14 elephants.
The famous 'Easter Island heads' actually have full bodies hidden underground, with arms and tummies that diggers found buried by centuries of dirt.
The island was made by three sleeping volcanoes that pushed up through the sea, leaving big round craters that are now filled with reedy lakes.
Rapa Nui kids learn two languages at school: Spanish, because the island belongs to Chile, plus Rapa Nui, a Polynesian language related to Maori and Hawaiian.
thingsToDo: Watch the sun rise behind Ahu Tongariki, where 15 huge moai stand shoulder-to-shoulder facing the island.
Wander the grassy slopes of Rano Raraku, the volcanic quarry where the moai were carved out of stone.
Build sandcastles on Anakena Beach and visit Ahu Nau Nau, a row of moai right next to the palm trees.
Climb up to Orongo, an old stone-hut village perched on the rim of the Rano Kau volcano crater.
Visit Ahu Akivi to meet the seven moai that face the sea instead of looking inland like all the others.
Touch the smooth round magic stone at Te Pito Kura, said by Rapa Nui legend to be the navel of the world.

wildlife.name: Red-tailed Tropicbird
wildlife.description: White seabird with two long red tail feathers that streams behind it like ribbons in the wind.

wildlife.name: Masked Booby
wildlife.description: Big white seabird with a black mask around its face that dives like an arrow for fish.

wildlife.name: Green Sea Turtle
wildlife.description: Friendly ocean reptile that swims in the warm bay at Anakena Beach and munches sea grass.

wildlife.name: Feral Horse
wildlife.description: Wild horse that roams freely across the grassy hills of Rapa Nui, often grazing near the moai.

localWord.meaning: Hello and goodbye in Rapa Nui, the Polynesian language of Easter Island.

images.alt: A row of giant stone moai statues stand at Ahu Tongariki facing inland.
Huge moai heads poke up out of the grassy hillside at the Rano Raraku quarry where the statues were carved.
A row of moai stand on Ahu Nau Nau on the white sand of Anakena Beach.
Round stone huts of the old Orongo ceremonial village on the rim of the Rano Kau volcano.
The seven moai of Ahu Akivi standing in a row, facing the ocean.
The smooth round magic stone of Te Pito Kura inside a low circle of rocks.
A red-tailed tropicbird sits on its nest, showing two long red tail feathers.
A masked booby seabird with a white body and dark mask around its eyes.
A green sea turtle glides through clear blue ocean water.
Free-range horses graze on the open grassy hills of Rapa Nui.
